2606.09204.title: The Injection Paradox: Brand-Level Suppression in Safety-Trained LLM Recommendations via RAG Context Injection
2606.09204.paper_summary: We present a reproducible failure mode of safety training in RAG-based LLM recommendation -- the Injection Paradox -- in which prompt injections embedded in retrieved documents backfire against the attacker, suppressing the target brand below the injection-free baseline. In safety-trained Claude models, documents containing prompt injections suffer a sharp drop in recommendation rate, and this suppression propagates beyond the injected document to unmodified documents of the same brand. In Claude Opus 4.6, the target brand drops from a 54% baseline to zero top-2 recommendations across all 50 trials, even though only 1 of 4 brand documents in the corpus contains an injection. The directional pattern is reproduced in counterfactual experiments and across three brands. A contrasting result across the GPT models tested, where the same injection instead increases recommendations, suggests model-family differences in how injection-like context affects recommendation behavior. These findings raise the technical possibility of a reverse-attack scenario in which an adversary embeds injections in a competitor's documents to suppress the competitor's brand via safety-sensitive model behavior.
2606.09204.paper_summary_zh: 我們展示了一種可重現的安全訓練失效模式，該模式發生在基於RAG的LLM推薦中——注入悖論——在這種情況下，嵌入在檢索文檔中的提示注入會對攻擊者造成反效果，將目標品牌的推薦率壓制在無注入的基準之下。 在經過安全訓練的Claude模型中，包含提示注入的文檔的推薦率急劇下降，這種壓制甚至擴展到同一品牌的未修改文檔。 在Claude Opus 4.6中，目標品牌的推薦率從54%的基準下降到所有50次試驗中零的前兩名推薦，儘管在語料庫中只有四個品牌文檔中的一個包含注入。 這種方向性模式在反事實實驗中以及三個品牌中得到了重現。 在測試的GPT模型中出現的對比結果，表明相同的注入反而增加了推薦，這暗示了模型家族在注入類上下文如何影響推薦行為方面的差異。 這些發現提出了一種技術上的可能性，即反攻擊場景，其中對手在競爭對手的文檔中嵌入注入，以通過安全敏感的模型行為壓制競爭對手的品牌。
2606.09204.author: Hyunseok Paeng et.al.
